macro_events.event_summary: Oil spikes over 3% as Iran and Israel exchange direct missile strikes, raising Middle East war risk and potential supply disruption through the Strait of Hormuz.
primary_drivers: Geopolitical risk premium added to crude prices
Threat to ~20% of global oil flows transiting Strait of Hormuz
Flight-to-safety into defense, gold, and US-domestic energy producers

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with large upstream exposure; earnings leverage to Brent moves with limited Middle East asset risk relative to peers. Direct beneficiary of higher realized crude prices.
Upstream-heavy integrated; every $5/bbl Brent move adds meaningful EPS. US/Kazakhstan/Permian production keeps cash flow geared to price spike with minimal Iran/Israel asset exposure.
Highest crude-price beta among US large-cap E&Ps due to Permian pure-play and leveraged balance sheet; classic spike trade vehicle (Buffett-owned, high retail/institutional flow magnet).
Pure-play Permian producer with no international/Middle East exposure; high operating leverage to WTI. Trades almost as a call option on oil price spikes.
Maker of PAC-3, THAAD, and Javelin systems actively used by Israel and likely to see replenishment orders; direct revenue link to Middle East munitions consumption.
Produces Patriot interceptors and AMRAAM used in Israeli/regional air defense; missile defense expendables get drawn down in active exchanges, driving FMS replenishment orders.
Prime on Israel's Arrow-3 program partnership and supplier of long-range strike/ISR systems; direct beneficiary of elevated Middle East defense spending.

losers.rationale: Jet fuel is ~20-25% of operating costs; a sustained crude spike compresses margins directly, and Delta lacks the fuel hedging cushion it once had.
Highest international/long-haul exposure among US carriers including Middle East routings (TLV, Dubai connections); double-hit from fuel costs and route disruption/diversions.
Most leveraged balance sheet among legacy carriers makes earnings most sensitive to a fuel cost shock; unhedged exposure to spot jet fuel.
Domestic-focused but jet fuel is largest single cost; Southwest unwound most of its famous hedges, leaving margin fully exposed to crude spike.
Marine bunker fuel costs spike with crude; Carnival also faces itinerary disruption risk for Eastern Mediterranean/Red Sea cruises if conflict widens.
Jet fuel and diesel are major input costs for the express/ground network; oil spike directly compresses operating margin in a quarter already under pressure.
Commodity chemicals producer with naphtha/ethane feedstock costs tied to crude; margin squeeze if oil sustains higher and downstream pricing lags.

headlines: Oil prices spike over 3% as Iran and Israel trade strikes, escalating regional tensions
